subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Airborne vs Droplet Transmission
description: A short lesson to help you apply Airborne vs Droplet Transmission.

hook.text: A sneeze launches 40,000 droplets at 100 mph. Some fall. Some float for hours. The difference matters.

buildup.text: Large droplets fall within 6 feet—flu and cold viruses spread this way through direct contact.

discovery.text: Airborne particles are smaller than 5 microns and float in air currents across entire rooms.

twist.text: Measles virus can linger airborne in a room for 2 hours after an infected person leaves.

climax.text: Airborne diseases require N95 respirators. Droplet diseases only need standard surgical masks.

punchline.text: Big drops fall fast. Tiny particles float. Size decides how far it flies.

quiz.question: What makes airborne transmission different from droplet transmission?
options: Airborne particles are smaller and can float in air for hours
Droplet diseases only spread through touch
